results.rendered_html: snāna n. (ifc. f(ā). ) bathing, washing, ablution, religious or ceremonial lustration (as of an idol &c.), bathing in sacred waters (considered as one of the six daily duties [cf. ṣaṭ-karman] or as an essential part of some ceremonial, esp. the ablutions performed by a Brahma-cārin on becoming a householder, cf. snātaka),  GṛŚrS. ;  Mn. ;  MBh.  &c. ( IW. 242 ;  RTL. 394 n. 1  &c.)
